title: Notice register in native content
subtitle: Casual, neutral, professional.
overview: Module: Native Content Skills. B2 Med strengthens abstract discussion, source response, professional communication, precise style, revision, and independent output.

sections.title: B2 med focus
rows: Register helps you know where a phrase belongs.
语域帮助你知道一个短语适合哪里。
B2 Med frame: identify the point, notice tone and structure, extract useful language, and respond independently.
B2 Med 框架：识别观点、注意语气和结构、提取有用语言，并独立回应。
Compare two phrases with different register.
比较两个语域不同的短语。
“I get what you mean” sounds casual and friendly, while “I understand your concern” sounds more professional and careful. Both are useful, but not in the same situation.
“I get what you mean” 听起来随意友好，而 “I understand your concern” 听起来更职业、更谨慎。两者都有用，但不适合同一情境。

items.en: Register helps you know where a phrase belongs.
items.zh: 语域帮助你知道一个短语适合哪里。

items.en: B2 Med frame: identify the point, notice tone and structure, extract useful language, and respond independently.
items.zh: B2 Med 框架：识别观点、注意语气和结构、提取有用语言，并独立回应。

items.en: “I get what you mean” sounds casual and friendly, while “I understand your concern” sounds more professional and careful. Both are useful, but not in the same situation.
items.zh: “I get what you mean” 听起来随意友好，而 “I understand your concern” 听起来更职业、更谨慎。两者都有用，但不适合同一情境。

items.en: I will make the idea precise before I try to make it sound advanced.
items.zh: 我会先让意思准确，再尝试让它听起来高级。

items.en: A strong B2 answer usually includes a condition, a limitation, or a fair counterpoint.
items.zh: 强 B2 回答通常包括条件、限制或公平的反方观点。

items.en: If the wording feels too abstract, I will add one concrete example from real life.
items.zh: 如果措辞感觉太抽象，我会加入一个来自真实生活的具体例子。

items.en: I can revise my answer for logic, tone, and rhythm.
items.zh: 我可以从逻辑、语气和节奏上修改我的回答。

items.en: This lesson is complete only when I can use the idea in my own words.
items.zh: 只有当我能用自己的话使用这个想法时，这节课才算完成。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Compare two phrases with different register.
lines.zh: 比较两个语域不同的短语。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: “I get what you mean” sounds casual and friendly, while “I understand your concern” sounds more professional and careful. Both are useful, but not in the same situation.
lines.zh: “I get what you mean” 听起来随意友好，而 “I understand your concern” 听起来更职业、更谨慎。两者都有用，但不适合同一情境。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Can you make the answer more precise or more concrete?
lines.zh: 你能让回答更准确或更具体吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes. I will narrow the claim, add one example, and mention one limitation.
lines.zh: 可以。我会缩小主张、加入一个例子，并提到一个限制。

items.en: Compare two phrases with different register.
items.zh: 比较两个语域不同的短语。

items.en: Answer once in six sentences with a clear structure.
items.zh: 用六句话做一次结构清楚的回答。

items.en: Add one B2 phrase such as that said, in practice, arguably, or the real issue is.
items.zh: 加入一个 B2 短语，比如 that said、in practice、arguably 或 the real issue is。

items.en: Record the answer, then rewrite the strongest sentence more naturally.
items.zh: 录下回答，然后把最强的一句改得更自然。

items.en: Use the revised sentence tomorrow in a different topic.
items.zh: 明天在不同话题中使用修改后的句子。
